body.title: Goal-as-queue-consumer — Igor reads queue, picks top ticket, fires goal_adopt during idle
body.result: Already fully implemented in prior session. read_queue_top() + adopt_top_queue_ticket() in ops.py; PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN habit seeded (cognitive, 30min schedule, code_ref ops:adopt_top_queue_ticket, active-goal guard); test_queue_consumer.py exists. Igor drains his own backlog autonomously during idle cycles.
body.description: Igor can't act on ticketed work autonomously because tickets live in queue.json, not his TWM. The PROC_GOAL_CONTINUATION loop exists but requires a human to trigger goal_adopt first. Missing: the loop that reads the queue during idle time and fires goal_adopt without a human saying 'work ticket T-xxx'. Implementation: (1) new tool read_queue_top() reads queue.json filtered to status=pending, sorted by priority ASC + created_at ASC, returns top ticket or None. (2) New habit PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN (30min scheduler): calls read_queue_top() — if ticket found AND no active GOAL currently in cortex → fires goal_adopt(ticket_id). Effect: Igor drains his own backlog during boredom/idle cycles. Pairs with PROC_BOREDOM_TRIGGER: boredom = idle + no queue work. Prerequisites: T-goal-close-habit (done — closes goals cleanly), PROC_GOAL_CONTINUATION (done — drives mechanical steps). Related: T-phase-d-canonical.
